skill.name: writing-to-logseq
skill.description: Write structured notes, pages, blocks, properties, and tags to a Logseq graph through the Logseq HTTP API. Includes helper operations, examples, templates, and safety guidance for controlled graph updates.
skill.summary: Create and update Logseq pages, blocks, tags, and properties through the local HTTP API.
skill.author: C0ntr0lledCha0s

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak-crypto patterns, but these are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline API names, and ordinary documentation text. The confirmed risk is expected for this skill: a bundled Python helper reads a Logseq URL and token, sends authenticated HTTP requests, and can create, update, or delete graph content. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Logseq API Write Access
medium_findings.description: The helper reads a configurable API URL and token, then sends JSON-RPC style write requests with an Authorization bearer header. This is legitimate for Logseq automation, but a wrong or untrusted URL could receive the token and requested note content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network call and bearer token header are directly visible in the helper. The default URL is localhost, so the risk depends on user configuration rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Logseq Operations Are Exposed
medium_findings.description: The skill exposes page and block deletion methods and documents delete workflows. The code checks that targets exist, but successful calls can still remove user graph data when requested.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Delete methods and safety guidance are explicit. The operations are expected for CRUD support, but the data loss impact is real if used incorrectly.

low_findings.title: Environment and Local Config Token Loading
low_findings.description: The helper reads LOGSEQ_API_URL, LOGSEQ_API_TOKEN, and an optional local config file. This is expected configuration handling, but tokens should be treated as sensitive credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment and config reads are direct and limited to Logseq API configuration. I found no evidence that the token is read for unrelated purposes.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The many external command alerts point to Markdown code fences, inline function names, and Python examples. I did not find shell backtick execution or command construction in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation examples, not executable shell commands. The actual Python helper uses urllib requests, not subprocess or shell execution.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto and Sensitive File Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography and Windows SAM detections do not match the semantic context of the cited content. The reviewed lines are skill descriptions, project templates, or Logseq system property examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious static labels do not correspond to cryptographic code or Windows credential files in these locations. No related malicious behavior was found nearby.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bearer Token Sent to Configurable HTTP Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper posts to the configured Logseq API URL and includes the API token in an Authorization bearer header. Users should keep the default localhost endpoint or verify any custom URL.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code shows the configurable URL and bearer header directly. This is a real pattern, but it supports the intended Logseq API workflow.

dangerous_patterns.title: Graph Content Deletion Methods
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper implements page and block deletion through Logseq editor API methods. These methods should require clear user intent because they can remove graph data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Deletion methods are explicit and call Logseq delete APIs after checking target existence. Their risk is data loss rather than hidden execution.

content.value_statement: Manual note transfer into Logseq can lose structure and context. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create organized pages, blocks, tags, and properties through the Logseq HTTP API.
actual_capabilities: Creates new Logseq pages with optional initial content and page properties.
Adds, updates, appends, and deletes blocks through Logseq editor API methods.
Sets or removes block properties, including simple typed values such as numbers and checkboxes.
Adds and removes tags from existing block content.
Syncs conversation notes into timestamped pages under a configurable page prefix.
Provides reusable page templates for meetings, tasks, daily notes, books, and knowledge articles.
limitations: Requires the Logseq HTTP API to be enabled and reachable from the local environment.
Requires a valid Logseq API token from an environment variable, parameter, or local config file.
Can modify or delete graph data when destructive operations are requested.
Does not include interactive conflict resolution for duplicate pages or concurrent edits.

use_cases.title: Capture Meeting Notes
use_cases.description: Create structured meeting pages with attendees, agenda items, notes, and follow-up sections.

use_cases.title: Organize Research Notes
use_cases.description: Save summaries, references, tags, and related pages into a Logseq knowledge graph.

use_cases.title: Sync AI Conversation Summaries
use_cases.description: Store important conversation outcomes as timestamped Logseq notes for later review.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Logseq page named "Project Notes" with a short overview and three starter bullet points.

prompt_templates.title: Append Daily Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Add content without replacing existing notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Append these meeting decisions to my Daily Notes page and keep the content as clear Logseq bullets.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Tagged Task Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Logseq task page for the release checklist with status, priority, due date, subtasks, and relevant tags.

prompt_templates.title: Sync Conversation Knowledge
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize this conversation into a Logseq knowledge page, add properties, link related concepts, and avoid duplicate pages.

output_examples.input: Create a meeting notes page for the planning call with Alice and Jordan.
output_examples.output: A Logseq meeting page is created with date, attendees, agenda, notes, action items, and follow-up sections.

output_examples.input: Append these decisions to Daily Notes and tag them as product and review.
output_examples.output: The Daily Notes page receives a new block with the decisions, timestamped context, and the requested tags.

output_examples.input: Save this conversation summary under Claude Notes with related page links.
output_examples.output: A timestamped Claude Notes page is updated with a summary, key points, follow-up questions, and Logseq page links.

best_practices: Use append and get-or-create operations before replacing existing Logseq content.
Confirm page titles, block UUIDs, and property names before update or delete operations.
Test bulk writes on a test graph and keep batch sizes small.
anti_patterns: Do not delete pages or blocks without explicit user confirmation.
Do not point the API URL at an untrusted server because the token is sent as a bearer credential.
Do not use broad automated writes when duplicate pages or conflicting edits are possible.

faq.question: Does this skill require Logseq to be running?
faq.answer: Yes. The Logseq HTTP API must be enabled and reachable before write operations can work.

faq.question: Where does the API token come from?
faq.answer: The helper can use a token parameter, LOGSEQ_API_TOKEN, or a local Logseq expert config file.

faq.question: Can it delete Logseq data?
faq.answer: Yes. The included operations support page and block deletion, so destructive requests need confirmation.

faq.question: Can it create reusable page structures?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes templates for meetings, tasks, daily notes, books, and knowledge base articles.

faq.question: Does it send data to cloud services?
faq.answer: No cloud endpoint is hardcoded. It posts to the configured Logseq API URL, which defaults to localhost.

faq.question: Is it safe for shared graphs?
faq.answer: Use caution. Shared graphs can have naming conflicts and concurrent edits, so verify targets before writing.
